file_name: BOLIVARMININGCORP_05_23_2003-EX-2.1-VISP WEB SITE BUILDING AND HOSTING AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is made on this 12th day of May 2003 by and between YourNetPlus.com, Inc., a New York Corporation; with its principle office located at 501 Route 208, Monroe, NY 10950 ("Provider") and Kingdom Connect, Inc., a Corporation with its principle office located at 1045 Stephanie Way, Minden, NV 89423 ("KCI").
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is made on this 12th day of May 2003 by and between YourNetPlus.com, Inc., a New York Corporation; with its principle office located at 501 Route 208, Monroe, NY 10950 ("Provider") and Kingdom Connect, Inc., a Corporation with its principle office located at 1045 Stephanie Way, Minden, NV 89423 ("Client").
perturbation.explanation: The term 'KCI' is initially defined as 'KCI' but later referenced as 'Client.' This introduces ambiguity as to whether 'KCI' and 'Client' are interchangeable terms, leading to potential confusion regarding the responsibilities and rights assigned to each entity throughout the agreement, specifically in section 3 regarding provider's responsibilities to KCI vs section 4 regarding KCI responsibilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PROVIDER'S RESPONSIBILITIES Provider agrees to provide KCI with VISP Private labeled website building and maintenance capabilities. Deliverables shall include: Sales collateral materials including brochures and flyers to help KCI sign customers to the service. A fully staffed back end including; Billing, real time credit card transactions, administrative reports, Customer and technical support, Online Customer Account Maintenance, Private labeled E-Mail Server, Administrative E-Mail Addresses, vacation e-mail, Web based and POP Access, Online Interfaces, a private labeled Internet portal for KCI and include an affiliate program. KCI's Customers will receive an initial VISP web site setup including 5Mbs of storage space, maintenance of the site, technical support, and any services necessary for use of the site for no charge.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'KCI' as 'KCI,' but the changed text redefines 'KCI' as 'Client.' The contradicted text refers to 'KCI' in the context of providing services. By changing the definition of 'KCI' to 'Client' in the initial definition, it creates a contradiction because the agreement now presents ambiguity as to which entity ('KCI' or 'Client') the 'Provider' is responsible for providing services, thus creating a direct conflict with section where KCI is referenced.

perturbation.original_text: KCI's Customers will receive an initial VISP web site setup including 5Mbs of storage space, maintenance of the site, technical support, and any services necessary for use of the site for no charge.
perturbation.changed_text: Client's End Users will receive an initial VISP web site setup including 5Mbs of storage space, maintenance of the site, technical support, and any services necessary for use of the site for no charge.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'KCI's Customers' to 'Client's End Users' creates ambiguity. The contract does not explicitly define 'End Users', leading to confusion. It is unclear if 'End Users' are the same as 'customers of KCI' as originally intended. If they are not the same it changes the scope of section 3 and creates a contradiction, especially with section 4 where KCI has responsibilities to clients. Are KCI's Clients equivalent to End Users?
perturbation.contradicted_text: KCI'S RESPONSIBILITIES To market Private Labeled Service to retail and wholesale clients of KCI. KCI, its officers, directors, employees and any authorized sub-licensee will (I) conduct their business in an honest, professional and ethical manner and (ii) not commit any action or omission to act which could adversely affect PROVIDER, its name, reputation or ability to conduct its business. KCI shall promptly and fully cooperate with Provider to address and resolve all issues, problems, administrative procedures, End User complaints, regulatory investigations or inquiries or any other circumstances arising from KCI's use of Provider's services.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'KCI's Customers' to 'Client's End Users' introduces ambiguity regarding who is entitled to the free website setup (5Mbs of storage space, maintenance of the site, technical support, and any services necessary for use of the site for no charge). The contract does not define 'End Users', and it's unclear if they are the same as 'retail and wholesale clients of KCI' as mentioned in the contradicted text. This ambiguity creates a contradiction because KCI has responsibilities to its 'clients' as outlined in the contradicted text. If 'End Users' are not the same as 'KCI's Clients', it is unclear if KCI is obligated to provide a free website to them, creating a direct conflict and uncertainty about KCI's obligations, which adheres to the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The parties understand and agree that the terms and conditions of this Agreement, all documents and invoices and all communications between the parties regarding this Agreement or the Service to be provided as well as such information relevant to any other agreement between the parties (collectively "Confidential Information"), are strictly confidential between KCI and Provider.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties understand and agree that the terms and conditions of this Agreement, all documents and invoices and all communications between the parties regarding this Agreement or the Service to be provided as well as such information relevant to any other agreement between the parties (collectively "Private Information"), are strictly confidential between KCI and Provider.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Information' creates ambiguity. The contract defines 'Confidential Information' and outlines specific protections. By changing the term to 'Private Information' without redefining it, it is unclear whether the definition, protections, and obligations associated with 'Confidential Information' still apply. This creates uncertainty in the enforcement of the confidentiality clause, specifically regarding the actions KCI is restricted from taking after termination of the contract described in Section 5 and directly contradicts the second half of the paragraph that defines confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this Agreement, "Confidential Information" shall mean information in written or other tangible form specifically labeled as such when disclosed by a Party. Confidential Information shall remain the property of the disclosing Party. A Party receiving Confidential Information shall: (1) use or reproduce such information only when necessary to perform this Agreement; (2) provide at the least the same care to avoid disclosure or unauthorized use of such information as it provides to protect it's own Confidential Information and; (3) limit access to such information to it's employees or agents who need such information to perform this agreement. Not withstanding anything to the contrary contained herein, a Party shall be allowed to disclose Confidential Information pursuant to judicial or governmental order or if otherwise required to do so by law. KCI pledges that it will not circumvent the relationships among venders, providers and clients developed by Provider either directly or indirectly, during the contract period and for a period of up to 2 (two) years following termination of this contract
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Private Information', while the contradicted text explicitly defines 'Confidential Information' and outlines specific obligations related to it. By changing the terminology without redefining it, the change creates uncertainty about whether the original definition and the associated obligations still apply. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which relies on the defined term 'Confidential Information' for its enforceability, especially regarding KCI's actions after contract termination.
